headlines.headline: Qatar Plans to Rapidly Restart LNG Production After Hormuz Opens
headlines.description: Qatar is planning to rapidly boost liquefied natural gas production once the Strait of Hormuz reopens, aiming to restore most of its export capacity within two months, according to people familiar with the matter.

headlines.headline: RBA interest rates: Reserve Bank holds official cash rate at 4.35% as economy slows and unemployment rises
headlines.description: Reserve Bank of Australia decision on Tuesday comes after three consecutive cash rate hikes this year

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan hikes rates to highest since 1995 as yen languishes at historic lows
headlines.description: This is the BOJ's first hike since December, when it raised rates to its current level of 0.75%, also its highest level in over 30 years.

headlines.headline: Japan raises interest rate to highest since 1995
headlines.description: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

headlines.headline: Woodside Energy’s Louisiana LNG President Abruptly Departs
headlines.description: The top executive at Woodside Energy Group Ltd.’s $17.5 billion liquefied natural gas project in Louisiana has departed, effective Monday, just over a year after she was hired, according to a company memo seen by Bloomberg.

headlines.headline: Watch Warsh Caught Between Trump, Bond Market Bet on Rate Hikes - Bloomberg
headlines.description: Just three weeks into the job, Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is already facing an unusually high-stakes test. Inflation is roaring back at the fastest pace in three years. There’s growing dissent among the central bank’s policymakers. And investors have been dumping US Treasury bonds and pili...

headlines.headline: Warsh's Fed is likely to hold rates steady — what the leadership change could mean for your money
headlines.description: The Federal Reserve's June meeting, the first helmed by new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, may impact many consumer borrowing and savings rates down the road.

headlines.headline: U.S.-Iran deal explained: What we know — and what remains unresolved
headlines.description: Stocks rose after a preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement, but questions remain over Hormuz, Israel and what follows a potential signing in Geneva.

headlines.headline: SpaceX tokens are a bust on IPO day—but blame supply and demand, not crypto
headlines.description: Tokenized stocks didn’t pan out, but the underlying idea is sound.

headlines.headline: ‘The Vampire Lestat’ Explores Gabriella And Lestat’s Horrific Bond
headlines.description: The Vampire Lestat's unnerving depiction of Gabriella and Lestat's bond pulls the curtain back on the abuse he suffered as a child and how it shaped the man he became.

headlines.headline: Oil shock 'far from over' as analysts warn of new Middle East risk premium
headlines.description: Commodity analysts warn that oil price volatility is likely to persist, despite the U.S.-Iran peace agreement.

headlines.headline: Oil Flows Will Remain Uncertain
headlines.description: The market will fluctuate based on comments about the ceasefire, but also signs of whether and how fast shipping through the Straits will resume.

headlines.headline: Treasury yields slide as Iran deal drives rethink on Fed interest rate hikes
headlines.description: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — fell over 4 basis points to 4.441%.

headlines.headline: Traders Cut South Africa Rate-Hike Bets on Iran Peace Deal
headlines.description: Traders have scaled back bets on additional South African interest-rate hikes after a US-Iran peace deal sent oil prices tumbling, potentially reducing inflationary pressures.

headlines.headline: Lagarde Says ECB Has Started to See Second Round Effects
headlines.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said high energy prices are starting to feed through to other parts of the economy, according to an interview with France Culture.

headlines.headline: Zhipu surges 33% as Wall Street raises bets on China AI after Anthropic curbs
headlines.description: Shares of Chinese AI model developer Zhipu surged as Wall Street banks raised bets on the company's ability to capture global AI demand.

headlines.headline: LNG tanker heads toward Hormuz as deal lifts reopening hopes
headlines.description: LNG tanker nears Hormuz as US-Iran deal raises hopes for reopening the crucial waterway, impacting global gas prices.

headlines.headline: Copper Pops Higher as US-Iran Agreement Puts Bulls in Charge
headlines.description: Copper rose as an interim peace agreement between the US and Iran eased fears over global economic growth and stoked optimism for metals demand.

headlines.headline: Crude oil futures drop after Trump promises an Iran deal will be signed Friday
headlines.description: Oil prices had already fallen quite dramatically on Thursday and Friday, in anticipation of an imminent deal. President Trump has posted online that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen after the deal is signed on Friday.

headlines.headline: Markets cheer U.S.-Iran agreement, but some investors caution deal is yet to be signed
headlines.description: Asian stocks rallied Monday while oil prices tumbled after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a peace deal aimed at ending nearly four months of conflict.

headlines.headline: Stock prices soar and oil falls after deal is reached on ending the Iran war
headlines.description: Stock markets are rallying worldwide, and oil prices are easing after the United States and Iran reached a tentative deal to get the global flow of crude going again. The S&P 500 rose 1.5% Monday on hopes that this time, the announcement of an Iran-U.S. agree…

headlines.headline: Oil prices slide after US and Iran sign peace deal
headlines.description: Under the agreement, the key Strait of Hormuz waterway will be reopened, US President Donald Trump said.

headlines.headline: BOJ Set to Hike Rates to Highest Since 1995 Despite Ueda Absence
headlines.description: The Bank of Japan is widely expected to raise its benchmark interest rate to the highest level since 1995 at the first regular policy meeting ever held without the governor in attendance.

headlines.headline: Bailey Defends BOE Gilt Sales After Criticism From Farage
headlines.description: Governor Andrew Bailey defended the Bank of England’s buying and selling of UK government bonds following criticism of the policy, including from Nigel Farage’s populist Reform UK party.

headlines.headline: FOMC Chair Warsh Won't Cut Rates Soon, A More Hawkish Fed Looms
headlines.description: Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair may not shift rates soon, but hawkish policy amid inflation/oil shocks could pressure gold & silver. Read the full analysis here.

headlines.headline: Week Ahead: 7 G10 Central Banks Meet, BOJ To Hike, Warsh Chairs First FOMC, G7 Summit
headlines.description: After threatening a new round of intense bombing of Iran, the US relented and suggested an agreement was near. Read more here.

headlines.headline: June FOMC Preview: What To Watch In Warsh’s Inaugural Fed Meeting (DJI)
headlines.description: Preview the June 17 FOMC meeting under new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, with key takeaways on rate hike vs cut odds from current economic data—read now.

headlines.headline: Kevin Warsh Navigates A Hawkish Fed Shift
headlines.description: Improved economic momentum and elevated inflation suggest the Federal Reserve will acknowledge the possibility of a future rate hike via new forecasts.

headlines.headline: ECB's Lagarde Warns of Impact of Prolonged Energy Shock
headlines.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde says euro-zone inflation should return to the 2% target by the second half of 2027, warning that a prolonged war in Iran, and subsequent impact on energy prices, would be a drag on growth. She made the comments at a news conference in Frankfurt after...

headlines.headline: ECB hikes interest rates for first time since 2023 as Iran war ramps-up energy costs
CNBC Daily Open: ECB heads for a hike amid Iran energy price pressures
headlines.description: The European Central Bank is expected to make its first interest rate hike in nearly three years amid increasing inflationary pressure due to the Iran war.

headlines.description: Trade UVXY tactically, not long-term: learn why this leveraged VIX ETF decays, lags spikes, and needs strict stop-loss risk management—read now.

headlines.headline: Invesco Corporate Bond Fund Q1 2026 Commentary
headlines.description: Bond yields, though down from their highs, are still at attractive levels compared to the last 10 years, in our view. Read more here.

headlines.description: Track rising inflation forecasts, the CPI vs. PPI gap, and what it means for Fed rate hikes and markets. Click here to read what investors need to know.

headlines.headline: Oil prices rise more than $2 on Israel strikes on Lebanon
headlines.description: U.S. crude futures were up $2.57 at $93.11 per barrel.

headlines.headline: Oil prices rise after Iran fires missiles at Israel, escalating regional tensions
headlines.description: Oil rises on Monday amid heightened tensions in the Middle East, raising concerns that the conflict may drag on.

headlines.headline: BOE’s Bailey Warns of Possible AI Rationing on Capacity Limits
headlines.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey suggested that artificial intelligence may need to be rationed because a lack of energy capacity will restrain the ability for every economic sector to deploy the new technology.

headlines.headline: EM Stocks, Currencies Dragged Down by Korean Market Selloff
headlines.description: Emerging market assets fell for the third straight session, led by a selloff in South Korean equities as investor enthusiasm for artificial intelligence stocks waned.

headlines.headline: BOE’s Bailey Says Aging Society With Jobless Youth Toxic for UK
headlines.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey warned the UK economy faces a worrying combination of an aging population and rapidly rising levels of young people out of work and education.

headlines.headline: EM Currencies Advance on Iran Talks With Rand Leading Gains
headlines.description: Most emerging market currencies advanced outside Asia as hopes of a deal in the Middle East drove down oil prices and boosted risk sentiment.
